title: Tebnine hospital bears witness to ongoing war in southern Lebanon
subhead: Wounded people continue to pour into the emergency room of the only facility still operating near the 'yellow line.' After Washington and Tehran signed a memorandum of understanding, Donald Trump called on Israel to be 'more responsible' in Lebanon, where more than 3,800 people have been killed since March 2.

title: Trump and Iran's president sign framework agreement at Château de Versailles
On Donbas front, warehouse manager aims to improve daily life for Ukrainian soldiers
Trump's shift on Ukraine welcomed by G7 leaders
subhead: The American president indicated a tougher line against Moscow during the three-day meeting in France, which was hailed as a success by G7 leaders. After the summit, he accompanied French President Emmanuel Macron on a private tour of Versailles, followed by a candlelit dinner with 30 guests.

title: Insee lowers growth forecasts as French households bear brunt of Middle East crisis
subhead: The French economy is expected to move at a sluggish pace through 2026, with growth reaching just 0.7% by year's end instead of the previously anticipated 0.9%.

title: MMA in Le Monde: From clandestine beginnings in France to the spotlight in front of the White House
subhead: Mixed martial arts has just experienced unprecedented media exposure thanks to US President Trump. In France, this combat sport, whose name first appeared in 2006 in the newspaper, had to fight to earn respect.

title: The French villages left behind by the long-abandoned hovertrain project
subhead: In the Petite Beauce region, a derelict monorail runs for 18 km through farmland, fueling nostalgia for a bygone era of industrial splendor.

Weakened at home, Macron counts on 'his' last G7 to boost his diplomatic record
subhead: As his second term grows increasingly complex at home amid the killing of 11-year-old Lyhanna, the French president, who is chairing the G7 summit in Evian, is staking his legitimacy on an ambitious international agenda.

title: From insider trading in France to an American cell phone: How Spain tracked down former prime minister Zapatero
subhead: Judicial cooperation among several countries, including France and Switzerland, has helped uncover an international network relying on 'opaque financial channels,' which were allegedly used, among other things, to pay the former Socialist leader.

title: Trump welcomed at Versailles after G7: 'Macron has turned the château into a diplomatic extension of the Elysée'
subhead: In an interview, historian Fabien Oppermann said that the dinner planned for Wednesday highlights the French president's desire 'to impress his counterpart,' in hopes of preserving the transatlantic relationship at a time when ties with the US are especially strained.
